question_text: Which of the following statements about magnetic poles is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Every magnet has only one pole.

options.label: B
options.text: Every magnet has a north pole and a south pole.

options.label: C
options.text: Magnetic poles can be separated by breaking a magnet in half.

options.label: D
options.text: A magnet's south pole is always stronger than its north pole.

explanation: Every magnet always has two poles: a north pole and a south pole. If you break a magnet in half, each piece will have its own north and south pole—you cannot separate them.
